title: News Sentiment Regression Scorer (Yahoo RSS → FinBERT)
summary: A July 2025 arXiv backtesting study (arXiv:2507.03350) applied sentiment regression models to news articles on Dow Jones 30 stocks and found all models produced positive returns, with the regression approach achieving 50.63% over 28 months versus buy-and-hold. The key insight is that regression-scored sentiment (a continuous score) outperforms binary positive/negative classification — suggesting Decifer's existing Yahoo RSS news dimension should score articles on a continuous sentiment scale rather than a binary flag. FinBERT (a finance-domain BERT model) can be run locally for free and produces continuous sentiment scores ideal for this approach.
expected_impact: +50% return over 28 months in backtested Dow Jones 30 study vs Buy & Hold; practical Decifer estimate: +2–3% win rate on news-catalyst setups
